pages.full_text: Laon Cathedra built 1220 - 1264. Corners of towers cut away, so platforms for cows to look out.
photo_captions: Laon Cathedra built 1220 - 1264. Corners of towers cut away, so platforms for cows to look out.
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in postcard/photo of Laon Cathedral (labelled 'Laon' on the postcard itself) with a handwritten caption below. A small decorative wheat/grain sticker is present. No handwritten page number visible at top-left. 'Cathedra' appears to be the writer's abbreviation for 'Cathedral'.

pages.full_text:
We drove thro the Laonnaise hills, skirting Champagne, then crossed a series of rivers such as Aisne, Marne + Seine on Droats. Stopped at a le Clerks. Reached Trainel Municipal Park -- rundown but clean.

Gorgeous chocolate
pages.notes: Page number 73 visible top-right. A glued-in Suchard Lait chocolate wrapper (red foil) occupies most of the page. 'Gorgeous chocolate' is written vertically in blue ink along the right margin beside the wrapper. 'Droats' likely refers to D-roads (French departmental roads). 'le Clerks' is uncertain — could be a place name or abbreviation for a shop/store; possibly 'le Clerc' (a French supermarket chain) or a locality name. 'Laonnaise hills' refers to the hills/plateau around Laon. 'Trainel' is a commune in Aube, France.
